author: .paulharris
content:
I've started looking at graffiti watermarks slightly differently, because i can report percentages as 'percentage of total mainnet blocks' for each client based on detected watermarks...

Over the last few days it looks like this:
```
Detection Summary (Last 4 Days)
--------------------------------------------------
Total blocks:                         28,660
Blocks with detected client:          14,029
Blocks without detected client:       14,631
Detected percentage:                  48.95%
Undetected percentage:                51.05%

Client Distribution (Last 4 Days)
--------------------------------------------------
Total blocks (last 4 days): 28,660

Consensus Clients:
  Client                    |           Count | Percentage
  -------------------------------------------------------
  Lighthouse                |           7,465 |     26.05%
  Teku                      |           3,158 |     11.02%
  Prysm                     |           3,120 |     10.89%
  Lodestar                  |             169 |      0.59%
  Grandine                  |              64 |      0.22%

Execution Clients:
  Client                    |           Count | Percentage
  -------------------------------------------------------
  go-ethereum               |           6,423 |     22.41%
  Nethermind                |           4,937 |     17.23%
  Besu                      |           2,358 |      8.23%
  Reth                      |             281 |      0.98%
  Erigon                    |              23 |      0.08%

```
Now that prysm is reporting, we've got nearly 50% coverage of mainnet blocks, and it's probably fair to make rough comparisons like 'prysm and teku are similar network percentages', and 'lighthouse is a significantly higher percentage than teku or prysm'.

Actual percentages are harder to derive, but its starting to become more interesting, so figured it's worth sharing.

author: manunlp
content:
I have this as well, based on graffiti:
https://clients-diversity.nalepa.org/

author: manunlp
content:
- Lodestar share is undervalued, because the client identification is dismissed if a custom graffiti is set.
- Nimbus does not implement client identification.
- Prysm V7.1.4 (the first version of Prysm reporting client identification on graffitis) uses `PR` instead of `PM`. It has been fixed on Prysm V7.1.5. This probably explains the diff between what you have and what I have.
